tools.name: tavo_status
tools.description: Return Tavo MCP server status and data-library asset counts.

tools.name: tavo_character_search
tools.description: Search Tavo assets by name. Use this before get/update when the id is unknown.

tools.name: tavo_character_get
tools.description: Read one Tavo asset by id. Call this before updating so unknown fields can be preserved.

tools.name: tavo_character_create
tools.description: Write a Tavo data-library asset. Supports dryRun, expectedRevision, and clientRequestId.

expectedRevision.description: Reject the write if the current asset revision differs.

clientRequestId.description: Idempotency key for retrying non-dry-run writes.

tools.name: tavo_chat_search
tools.description: Search Tavo chats by display title.

tools.name: tavo_chat_get
tools.description: Read one Tavo chat by id.

tools.name: tavo_chat_create
tools.description: Create a Tavo chat from character/persona ids.

tools.name: tavo_chat_update
tools.description: Update chat metadata, members, and overrides.

tools.name: tavo_chat_delete
tools.description: Delete a Tavo chat through provider cleanup.

tools.name: tavo_chat_copy
tools.description: Copy a Tavo chat through provider clone logic.

tools.name: tavo_chat_reset
tools.description: Reset a Tavo chat messages/state.

tools.name: tavo_current_chat_get
tools.description: Read the current active Tavo chat.

tools.name: tavo_current_chat_set
tools.description: Set the current active Tavo chat.

tools.name: tavo_message_find
tools.description: Find messages in a Tavo chat.

tools.name: tavo_message_get
tools.description: Read one message by stable id or drifting index. Id wins.

tools.name: tavo_message_count
tools.description: Count messages in a Tavo chat.

tools.name: tavo_message_append
tools.description: Append a message to a Tavo chat.

tools.name: tavo_message_insert
tools.description: Insert a message at a chat index.

tools.name: tavo_message_update
tools.description: Update a message by stable id or index.

tools.name: tavo_message_delete
tools.description: Delete a message by stable id or index.

tools.name: tavo_input_get
tools.description: Read the current active chat input box text.

tools.name: tavo_input_set
tools.description: Replace the current active chat input box text.

tools.name: tavo_input_append
tools.description: Append text to the current active chat input box.

tools.name: tavo_input_clear
tools.description: Clear the current active chat input box.

tools.name: tavo_input_send
tools.description: Send the current active chat input box through the normal UI chat flow.

tools.name: tavo_plugin_search
tools.description: Search installed Tavo plugins by id, name, or description.

tools.name: tavo_plugin_get
tools.description: Read one installed Tavo plugin, including manifest and config.

tools.name: tavo_plugin_package
tools.description: Package plugin source files into an installable zip.

tools.name: tavo_plugin_install
tools.description: Install a Tavo plugin from zipBase64 or a local zipPath.

tools.name: tavo_plugin_uninstall
tools.description: Uninstall an installed Tavo plugin.

tools.name: tavo_plugin_set_enabled
tools.description: Enable or disable an installed plugin.

tools.name: tavo_plugin_set_config
tools.description: Set one plugin configuration value.

tools.name: tavo_plugin_reset_config
tools.description: Reset one plugin configuration to manifest defaults.

tools.name: tavo_plugin_validate_manifest
tools.description: Validate a plugin manifest object before packaging or installing.

tools.name: tavo_plugin_get_runtime_contributions
tools.description: List enabled plugin runtime contributions exposed to Tavo.

contents.text: # Tavo MCP Server¥n¥nTavo exposes a Streamable HTTP MCP endpoint for generic MCP clients and agent applications. Use it to inspect capabilities, read Tavo resources, and call tools that manage data-library assets, chats, messages, the active input box, and plugins.¥n¥n## Discovery¥n¥n1. Call `initialize`.¥n2. Call `tools/list` and treat the returned tool descriptors as canonical.¥n3. Call `resources/list` and `resources/templates/list` to discover documentation, schemas, runtime state, and asset resources.¥n4. Read `tavo://docs/tools` for a grouped tool guide and `tavo://docs/write-safety` before writing.¥n5. Read `tavo://docs/macros` before writing prompt-like text for characters, lorebooks, regexes, presets, or messages.¥n6. Read `tavo://docs/tavojs` before writing JavaScript for character cards.¥n7. Read `tavo://docs/plugins` before packaging or editing Tavo plugins.¥n¥n## Transport¥n¥nRequests are JSON-RPC 2.0 over `POST /mcp` with `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.¥n¥nThe server supports request/response HTTP. It does not expose SSE subscriptions yet.¥n

# Tavo MCP Tools¥n¥nCall `tools/list` for the canonical machine-readable interface. Each tool descriptor includes `name`, `description`, and `inputSchema` for generic MCP clients.¥n¥n## Status¥n- `tavo_status`¥n¥n## Characters¥n- `tavo_character_search`¥n- `tavo_character_get`¥n- `tavo_character_create`¥n- `tavo_character_update`¥n- `tavo_character_import_card`¥n- `tavo_character_delete`¥n¥n## Lorebooks¥n- `tavo_lorebook_search`¥n- `tavo_lorebook_get`¥n- `tavo_lorebook_create`¥n- `tavo_lorebook_update`¥n- `tavo_lorebook_import`¥n- `tavo_lorebook_delete`¥n- `tavo_lorebook_entry_upsert`¥n- `tavo_lorebook_entry_delete`¥n¥n## Regex¥n- `tavo_regex_search`¥n- `tavo_regex_get`¥n- `tavo_regex_create`¥n- `tavo_regex_update`¥n- `tavo_regex_import`¥n- `tavo_regex_delete`¥n- `tavo_regex_entry_upsert`¥n- `tavo_regex_entry_delete`¥n- `tavo_regex_test`¥n¥n## Presets¥n- `tavo_preset_search`¥n- `tavo_preset_get`¥n- `tavo_preset_create`¥n- `tavo_preset_update`¥n- `tavo_preset_import`¥n- `tavo_preset_delete`¥n- `tavo_preset_entry_upsert`¥n- `tavo_preset_entry_delete`¥n- `tavo_preset_set_active`¥n¥n## Personas¥n- `tavo_persona_search`¥n- `tavo_persona_get`¥n- `tavo_persona_create`¥n- `tavo_persona_update`¥n- `tavo_persona_delete`¥n- `tavo_persona_set_active`¥n¥n## Chats¥n- `tavo_chat_search`¥n- `tavo_chat_get`¥n- `tavo_chat_create`¥n- `tavo_chat_update`¥n- `tavo_chat_delete`¥n- `tavo_chat_copy`¥n- `tavo_chat_reset`¥n- `tavo_current_chat_get`¥n- `tavo_current_chat_set`¥n¥n## Messages¥n- `tavo_message_find`¥n- `tavo_message_get`¥n- `tavo_message_count`¥n- `tavo_message_append`¥n- `tavo_message_insert`¥n- `tavo_message_update`¥n- `tavo_message_delete`¥n¥n## Input Box¥n- `tavo_input_get`¥n- `tavo_input_set`¥n- `tavo_input_append`¥n- `tavo_input_clear`¥n- `tavo_input_send`¥n¥n## Plugins¥n- `tavo_plugin_search`¥n- `tavo_plugin_get`¥n- `tavo_plugin_package`¥n- `tavo_plugin_install`¥n- `tavo_plugin_uninstall`¥n- `tavo_plugin_set_enabled`¥n- `tavo_plugin_set_config`¥n- `tavo_plugin_reset_config`¥n- `tavo_plugin_validate_manifest`¥n- `tavo_plugin_get_runtime_contributions`¥n¥nFor write tools, read `tavo://docs/write-safety` before calling `tools/call`.

# Tavo MCP Write Safety¥n¥nBefore updating or deleting an existing Tavo asset, call the matching search/get tool and preserve the returned `revision`.¥n¥nWrite tools commonly support:¥n¥n- `dryRun`: preview the diff without saving.¥n- `expectedRevision`: reject stale writes when the current revision differs.¥n- `clientRequestId`: make non-dry-run retries idempotent.¥n¥nTypical safe flow:¥n¥n1. Search or read the target asset.¥n2. Prepare the smallest payload needed for the change.¥n3. Call the write tool with `dryRun: true`.¥n4. Review the returned `diff`.¥n5. Call the write tool again with `expectedRevision` and a stable `clientRequestId`.¥n¥nMessage tools take explicit `chatId`. Message target tools accept stable `id` or 0-based `index`; `id` takes precedence.¥n

# Tavo Macros¥n¥nTavo macros are `{{...}}` placeholders expanded while rendering prompt-like text. Use them in character cards, lorebooks, regexes, presets, generated messages, and prompt templates when the value should follow the current chat instead of being fixed at authoring time.¥n¥n## Authoring Rule¥n¥nPrefer macros over hard-coded names or copied context. In particular, avoid hard-coding the current character name, persona/user name, group members, current scenario, or recent messages when a macro can express the same thing.¥n¥nGood:¥n¥n```text¥n{{char}} notices {{user}} hesitating.¥n```¥n¥nAvoid:¥n¥n```text¥nAlice notices Bob hesitating.¥n```¥n¥nThis keeps assets reusable when the user renames a character, changes persona, imports the asset into another chat, or uses the same lorebook or regex with multiple characters.¥n¥n## Identity and Group Macros¥n¥n- `{{char}}`: current character name or nickname.¥n- `{{user}}`: current persona/user name.¥n- `{{group}}`: all group-chat character names, comma-separated.¥n- `{{charIfNotGroup}}`: current character in single-character contexts; group names in group contexts.¥n- `{{groupNotMuted}}`: unmuted group-chat character names, comma-separated.¥n¥nUse these in character descriptions, first messages, lorebook entries, regex replacements, and preset prompts instead of literal names.¥n¥n## Character and Persona Context¥n¥n- `{{charPrompt}}`: character system prompt.¥n- `{{charInstruction}}`: character post-history instructions.¥n- `{{charDescription}}` / `{{description}}`: character description.¥n- `{{charPersonality}}` / `{{personality}}`: character personality.¥n- `{{charScenario}}`: character scenario.¥n- `{{scenario}}`: conversation override scenario, falling back to character scenario.¥n- `{{persona}}`: persona description.¥n- `{{mesExamples}}`: rendered example messages.¥n- `{{mesExamplesRaw}}`: raw example messages.¥n- `{{charVersion}}`: character version.¥n- `{{charDepthPrompt}}`: character depth prompt.¥n- `{{charCreatorNotes}}` / `{{creatorNotes}}`: creator notes.¥n- `{{charJailbreak}}`: legacy alias for post-history instructions.¥n¥n## Message Context¥n¥n- `{{lastMessage}}`: most recent visible message.¥n- `{{lastUserMessage}}`: most recent visible user message.¥n- `{{lastCharMessage}}`: most recent visible non-user message.¥n- `{{input}}`: most recent visible user message in generation context.¥n¥nOnly use recent-message macros when the text is intentionally dynamic. Static lore should usually refer to `{{char}}`, `{{user}}`, and the character/context macros instead.¥n¥n## Date, Random, and Formatting¥n¥n- `{{time}}`: current local time.¥n- `{{time::UTC+9}}` / `{{time::UTC-5}}`: time at a UTC hour offset.¥n- `{{date}}`: current local date.¥n- `{{weekday}}`: current local weekday.¥n- `{{isotime}}`: current time as `HH:mm`.¥n- `{{isodate}}`: current date as `yyyy-MM-dd`.¥n- `{{idleDuration}}`: duration since the prior relevant user message.¥n- `{{random::one,two,three}}`: random item from a comma-separated list.¥n- `{{roll::3d6}}` / `{{dice::3d6}}`: dice roll total.¥n- `{{newline}}` / `{{newline::3}}`: one or more newline characters.¥n- `{{space}}` / `{{space::4}}`: one or more spaces.¥n- `{{trim}}`: trim surrounding whitespace from the rendered text.¥n- `{{noop}}`: empty string.¥n- `{{//comment}}`: template comment; removed during rendering.¥n¥n## Variables¥n¥nChat-scoped variables travel with the chat export:¥n¥n- `{{setvar::name::value}}`: set a chat variable.¥n- `{{addvar::name::value}}`: append/add to a chat variable.¥n- `{{incvar::name}}`: increment a chat variable by 1.¥n- `{{decvar::name}}`: decrement a chat variable by 1.¥n- `{{getvar::name}}`: read a chat variable.¥n¥nGlobal variables persist across chats and should use collision-resistant names:¥n¥n- `{{setglobalvar::name::value}}`¥n- `{{addglobalvar::name::value}}`¥n- `{{incglobalvar::name}}`¥n- `{{decglobalvar::name}}`¥n- `{{getglobalvar::name}}`¥n¥nPrefer chat variables for roleplay state such as affection, health, inventory, location, or temporary flags. Use global variables only for values intentionally shared across unrelated chats.¥n¥n## Agent Checklist¥n¥nWhen writing or editing Tavo assets:¥n¥n1. In character cards, replace literal character/user names in descriptions, scenarios, first messages, examples, and instructions with `{{char}}` and `{{user}}` when they refer to the active names.¥n2. In lorebooks, write reusable entries with `{{char}}`, `{{user}}`, `{{group}}`, and `{{scenario}}` instead of chat-specific names.¥n3. In regexes, use macros in replacement text when the replacement should adapt to the active character, persona, group, time, or variables.¥n4. In presets and prompt templates, use context macros such as `{{persona}}`, `{{description}}`, `{{scenario}}`, and `{{mesExamples}}` instead of copying character-card fields.¥n5. Do not replace proper nouns that are part of the fictional world, a third-party character, a location, or a deliberate fixed name.¥n6. Read the current asset first and preserve existing macro style unless changing it is part of the user's request.¥n

# TavoJS Authoring Guide¥n¥nTavoJS is the JavaScript API exposed inside rendered chat content and character cards. Use it when a character card needs interactive UI, persistent state, message editing, generation, image generation, file storage, or input-box actions.¥n¥nFor plugin packages, read `tavo://docs/plugins`.¥n¥n## General Rules¥n¥n- Prefer chat-scoped data for roleplay state so exported chats carry their state.¥n- Use `global` scope only for truly cross-chat data and choose collision-resistant names.¥n- Use message scope for state attached to a single message/floor.¥n- Most APIs that return app data are async and should be called with `await`.¥n- Variable snapshot APIs such as `tavo.get`, `tavo.set`, and `tavo.unset` are synchronous from JavaScript.¥n- For destructive, costly, or external actions, expect Tavo confirmation settings to apply.¥n- Write small scripts that fail safely. If an optional object is missing, show a toast or no-op instead of throwing.¥n¥n## Common Namespaces¥n¥n- `tavo.get(name, scope)`, `tavo.set(name, value, scope)`, `tavo.unset(name, scope)`: persistent variables.¥n- `tavo.message.find(range, filter)`: read messages by 0-based floor range.¥n- `tavo.message.get(idOrQuery)`: read one message by stable `id` or `{ index }`.¥n- `tavo.message.current()`: read the message running the current script.¥n- `tavo.message.append(message)`, `tavo.message.update(message, opts)`, `tavo.message.delete(idOrQuery)`: edit chat messages.¥n- `tavo.chat.current()`, `tavo.chat.update(chat)`: read or update the active chat.¥n- `tavo.character.*`, `tavo.persona.*`, `tavo.preset.*`, `tavo.lorebook.*`, `tavo.regex.*`: manage data-library assets.¥n- `tavo.generate(prompt, options)`: run a one-off text generation.¥n- `tavo.image.generate(prompt, options)`: run a one-off image generation.¥n- `tavo.file.save/load/delete/exists/url`: persist text, base64, data URLs, or downloaded files.¥n- `tavo.input.get/set/append/clear/send`: control the active chat input box.¥n- `tavo.utils.toast/openUrl/select/export/preview`: common UI helpers.¥n- `tavo.app.version()` and `tavo.app.versionNumber()`: read app version.¥n¥n## Message Scope¥n¥nMessage variables live on one message and are removed when that message is deleted.¥n¥n```js¥ntavo.set('hp', 100, 'message')¥nconst hp = tavo.get('hp', 'message')¥ntavo.set('hp', 50, { scope: 'message', id: 2338 })¥ntavo.unset('hp', { scope: 'message', index: -1 })¥n```¥n¥nUse stable message `id` for round trips. Use `index` only for immediate floor-position access because indexes drift when messages are deleted or inserted.¥n¥n## Character Card Pattern¥n¥nPut UI in the message HTML and use TavoJS for behavior:¥n¥n```html¥n<button onclick=¥

>¥n¥n```¥n¥n## Agent Checklist¥n¥nWhen generating TavoJS for a user:¥n¥n1. Identify whether the script runs in a character card, an existing bubble, or a plugin UI fragment.¥n2. Store roleplay state in chat scope unless the user explicitly needs global state.¥n3. Use `tavo.message.current()` when the script needs to modify or inspect its own message.¥n4. Use `tavo.file.save(..., { scope: 'chat' })` instead of storing large images or blobs in variables.¥n5. For data-library edits, read the current object first, preserve unknown fields, and update only what changed.¥n6. Include user-visible error handling with `try/catch` and `tavo.utils.toast`.¥n

# Tavo Plugins¥n¥nTavo plugins are `.tpg` zip packages installed from Tavo Settings -> Plugins. Use plugins when behavior should be reusable across characters or chats. Use plain TavoJS inside character cards when the behavior belongs to one card.¥n¥nFor character-card JavaScript, read `tavo://docs/tavojs`.¥n¥n## Package Layout¥n¥n```text¥nmy-plugin.tpg¥n|-- manifest.json¥n|-- actions.js¥n|-- ui/¥n|-- icons/guide.png¥n`-- cover.png¥n```¥n¥n`manifest.json` must be at the package root. `scripts.actions` points to the¥noptional action-handler registration script, usually root `actions.js`.¥n¥n## manifest.json¥n¥nRequired fields:¥n¥n- `id`: reverse-domain style plugin id, lowercase letters/digits with `.`, `_`, or `-` separators.¥n- `name`: display name.¥n- `version`: version string; semantic versions like `1.0.0` are recommended.¥n¥nCommon optional fields:¥n¥n- `scripts.actions`: package-relative action-handler script path. Required only¥n  when the plugin declares `contributes.inputActions` or `contributes.sidebar`;¥n  invalid when the plugin declares neither native action type.¥n- `author`¥n- `description`¥n- `cover`: package-relative cover image path.¥n- `icon`: deprecated; ignored by current Tavo versions. Use¥n  `contributes.inputActions[].icon` for native input menu icons.¥n- `minAppVersion`¥n- `permissions`: capability namespaces requested by the plugin, such as¥n  `generate`, `variable`, `input`, `message`, `file`, or `network`. Current¥n  Tavo versions display these declarations as author intent; they are not yet¥n  enforced as runtime permission gates.¥n- `contributes`¥n¥n## External Resources and Paths¥n¥nPlugin resource fields are package-relative virtual paths and must use `/`¥nseparators on every platform, including Windows. Do not use OS path separators¥nor raw `path.join()` output in manifest paths. Do not use absolute paths, `..`¥ntraversal, Windows backslash paths, or `http(s)://` URLs for package resources.¥nMissing, unreadable, or undecodable resources fall back to the default UI or¥nare skipped.¥n¥nTavo currently does not enforce image pixel dimensions or file sizes at install¥ntime. Use these authoring guidelines to avoid mobile decode cost and menu jank:¥n¥n- `cover`: PNG, JPEG, or WebP. Prefer a square or near-square image such as¥n  `1024x1024` or `1125x1125`, up to about 1 MB. The plugin detail page fits it¥n  by width and aligns it to the top; the plugin list crops it into a circle.¥n- `contributes.inputActions[].icon`: PNG or WebP. Prefer a transparent square¥n  image, `64x64` or `128x128`; keep it at or below `512x512` and about 256 KB.¥n  The native menu scales it into a fixed icon slot.¥n- `contributes.htmlFragments[].src`: UTF-8 HTML file. Keep it lightweight and¥n  inline required CSS/JS where practical. Other files inside the plugin package¥n  are not automatically served as WebView static assets; use data URLs, Tavo¥n  file API outputs, or user-authorized remote URLs when an HTML fragment needs¥n  images or other subresources.¥n- `scripts.actions`: UTF-8 JavaScript file, usually root `actions.js`. It¥n  registers native action handlers and is not a plugin startup script.¥n¥n`.tpg` is a zip package; `.zip` is also accepted for development/import flows.¥nZip entries must not be absolute paths or traverse out of the plugin directory.¥n¥n## Contributions¥n¥n`contributes.inputActions` adds items to the chat input `+` menu:¥n¥n```json¥n{¥n  ¥

}¥n    ]¥n  }¥n}¥n```¥n¥nEach input action may set `icon` to a package-relative image path. If omitted or¥ninvalid, Tavo uses the default plugin icon. Prefer a square transparent¥n`64x64` or `128x128` PNG/WebP; avoid images larger than `512x512` or about¥n256 KB because Tavo scales the icon visually but oversized files still cost¥nmemory to decode.¥n¥nNative menu labels are single-line and ellipsized when they are too long.¥n¥n`contributes.sidebar` adds actions to the chat right sidebar:¥n¥n```json¥n{¥n  ¥

}¥n    ]¥n  }¥n}¥n```¥n¥nEach sidebar action uses:¥n¥n- `id`: stable action id used by the `scripts.actions` script to register the handler.¥n- `label`: text shown in the right-sidebar row.¥n¥nIf a plugin declares multiple sidebar actions, Tavo shows them in one section¥ntitled `Plugin ﾂｷ Plugin Name`. The first version does not support descriptions,¥nicons, dynamic state, chat-type filters, or embedded panels.¥n¥nWhen a plugin declares `contributes.inputActions` or `contributes.sidebar`, it¥nmust also set `scripts.actions`. When a user taps an input action or sidebar¥naction, Tavo dispatches it to a matching handler registered by that action¥nscript. Tavo loads this script only for native actions; it is not a plugin¥nstartup entry point.¥n¥n```js¥ntavo.plugin.onInputAction('guide', async () => {¥n  const input = await tavo.input.get();¥n  const basePrompt =¥n    tavo.plugin.config.get('basePrompt') ||¥n    'Draft a reply from this context:';¥n  const draft = await tavo.generate(`${basePrompt}¥n¥n${input}`);¥n  await tavo.input.set(draft);¥n});¥n¥ntavo.plugin.onSidebarAction('summary', async () => {¥n  await tavo.utils.toast('Summarizing chat');¥n});¥n```¥n¥nAction handlers run in the WebView advanced rendering runtime. If Advanced¥nRendering is disabled, the action remains visible in the native UI, but¥ntapping it shows a dialog guiding the user to enable Advanced Rendering and the¥nhandler is not executed.¥n¥nRuntime APIs available to plugin code:¥n¥nWhen Advanced Rendering is enabled, `scripts.actions`, chat HTML fragments,¥nsidebar actions, and input actions share the same scoped `tavo` facade:¥n¥n- `tavo.plugin.pluginId` and `tavo.plugin.fragmentId`; `fragmentId` is set for¥n  HTML fragments and `null` in the action runtime.¥n- `tavo.plugin.onInputAction(id, handler)`¥n- `tavo.plugin.onSidebarAction(id, handler)`¥n- `tavo.plugin.on('inputActions:<id>', handler)` and¥n  `tavo.plugin.on('sidebar:<id>', handler)` for lower-level event registration¥n- `tavo.plugin.config.get(key)` and `tavo.plugin.config.all()`¥n- `tavo.chat.current()` and `tavo.chat.update(patch)`¥n- `tavo.message.find(query)`, `get(id)`, `current()`, `count(query)`,¥n  `append(message)`, `update(id, patch)`, and `delete(id)`¥n- `tavo.input.get()`, `set(text)`, `append(text)`, `clear()`, `send()`¥n- `tavo.generate(prompt)` and `tavo.image.generate(prompt, options)`¥n- `tavo.memory.current()` and `tavo.memory.update(patch)`¥n- `tavo.character.*`, `tavo.persona.*`, `tavo.preset.*`, `tavo.lorebook.*`,¥n  and `tavo.regex.*` for data-library list/get/create/update/delete APIs¥n- `tavo.file.save(data, options)`, `load(id)`, `delete(id)`, `exists(id)`,¥n  and `url(id)`¥n- `tavo.utils.toast(text)`, `openUrl(url)`, `select(options)`, `export(data,¥n  options)`, and `preview(data, options)`¥n- `tavo.app.version()` and `tavo.app.versionNumber()`¥n- `tavo.get(key, scope)`, `set(key, value, scope)`,¥n  `update(key, patchOrUpdater, scope)`, and `unset(key, scope)`¥n¥n`tavo.message.current()` points to the rendered message only inside¥n`/messages` HTML fragments. It returns `null` in `/chat` fragments, input¥nactions, and sidebar actions. The message variable scope follows the same rule:¥n`tavo.get/set/update/unset(..., 'message')` maps to the current message only¥nwhen a current message exists.¥n¥nThe handler currently receives no arguments. If an action has no registered¥nhandler, Tavo reports a missing handler. If the handler throws, Tavo reports a¥nplugin action failure.¥n¥nHandlers may be async and may open browser-native dialogs such as `prompt`,¥n`confirm`, or `alert`. The native menu dispatch only waits for the handler to¥nstart successfully; the handler's async work continues inside the WebView¥nruntime.¥n¥n`contributes.htmlFragments` injects lightweight HTML into the Advanced¥nRendering WebView. Each item uses:¥n¥n- `id`: fragment id.¥n- `src`: package-relative UTF-8 HTML file path. It must not be a URL, absolute¥n  path, backslash path, or `..` traversal.¥n- `mount`: where to inject the fragment.¥n¥nSupported mounts:¥n¥n- `/chat` (`/chat/body/end`)¥n- `/chat/head/start`, `/chat/head/end`¥n- `/chat/body/start`, `/chat/body/end`¥n- `/messages` (`/messages/end`)¥n- `/messages/start`, `/messages/end`¥n- `/messages/start?role=user` or `role=character`¥n- `/messages/end?position=first` or `position=last`¥n¥nHTML fragments are installed plugin runtime code. If Advanced Rendering is¥ndisabled, fragments are not mounted. When Advanced Rendering is enabled,¥nfragment scripts run as plugin code and are not gated by the chat-content¥nJavaScript execution mode. The chat-content JavaScript mode only controls¥ncharacter-card, model-output, and other message bubble content.¥n¥nFragment scripts receive the same scoped `tavo` facade as action handlers.¥nFragments mounted under `/messages` also receive the current message context;¥nfragments mounted under `/chat` do not.¥n¥n`contributes.settings.schema` defines the plugin settings form. Supported element types:¥n¥n- `switch`¥n- `select`¥n- `slider`¥n- `text`¥n- `textarea`¥n- `info`¥n- `divider`¥n- `break`¥n¥nField elements such as `switch`, `select`, and `slider` use `key`, `label`, and optional `default`. `select` requires `options`. `slider` requires `min` and `max`.¥n¥n## Example Manifest¥n¥n```json¥n{¥n  ¥

}¥n      ]¥n    }¥n  }¥n}¥n```¥n¥n## MCP Tools¥n¥nUse MCP plugin tools to package, validate, install, inspect, enable, disable, configure, and uninstall plugins. Call `tools/list` for the exact schemas.¥n¥nTypical agent flow:¥n¥n1. Read `tavo://docs/plugins`.¥n2. Create `manifest.json`, `actions.js` when the plugin declares native actions, and optional assets.¥n3. Call `tavo_plugin_validate_manifest`.¥n4. Call `tavo_plugin_package`.¥n5. Call `tavo_plugin_install` with the returned `zipBase64`.¥n6. Call `tavo_plugin_set_enabled` if the user wants it enabled.¥n
